claim: The 3x suffix dealer mean drift between Brain-V's implementation (2.42-2.52 across hands) and Stolfi's (2000) published 0.80 is primarily driven by Brain-V's CRUST set including the 'circles' glyphs {a, o, y} and the letter {e}, which Stolfi treats as a separate CIRCLES category distinct from the dealer letters. When Brain-V's suffix dealer computation is recalculated using Stolfi's strict dealer set {d, l, r, s, n, x, i, m, g} (9 letters), the mean should approach Stolfi's 0.80 within 0.05 tolerance on the whole manuscript (Stolfi's scope), with remaining residual attributable to secondary drift sources (q handling, weirdo token exclusion, suffix definition).
evidence_for: Stolfi 2000 grammar (voynich.nu/hist/stolfi/grammar.html) enumerates CRUST as exactly {d, l, r, s, n, x, i, m, g} — 9 letters. The 'circles' {a, o, y} are in a separate category described as 'modifiers' that attach to the nearest non-circle letter. Brain-V's current CRUST set {d, l, r, s, n, x, i, m, g, q, o, y, a, e} adds 5 extra glyphs (q, o, y, a, e) that Stolfi does not count as dealers.

Stolfi explicitly states: 'the average lengths are 0.14 dealers in the prefix, 0.80 in the suffix, and 0.94 in the whole word.' His 0.80 is the unconditional suffix dealer mean on the WHOLE manuscript (not Currier B specifically); he does not split by Currier A/B.

If a typical Hand A/B suffix contains ~2.4 glyphs in Brain-V's CRUST but only ~0.8 of those are in Stolfi's DEALERS, the reduction factor is ~3x — matching the observed drift exactly. This is the null-prediction under pure categorisation drift.

Stolfi explicitly handles q separately: 'the letter q only occurs at the beginning of a normal word'; 'we could say that the q counts as 0.4 of a dealer.' Brain-V treats q as a full-weight crust member. Secondary drift source.
Stolfi excludes 'weirdo' (abnormal) tokens from the grammar analysis: '1295 tokens (3.7%) in the main text, 127 tokens (12.4%) in the labels'. Brain-V includes all tokens. Third-order drift source.
evidence_against: Stolfi's own tables have some imprecision: the summary 0.80 figure is from prose but the 2D table shows 0.81 conditional on prefix=0 and varying suffix-length averages. Our 0.05 tolerance may be tighter than the internal consistency of Stolfi's own published numbers.

The CIRCLES treatment is not a simple set-membership operation. Stolfi parses circles as 'modifiers' to the next non-circle letter, which changes the structure of the word (not just the count). A strict drop-circles-from-CRUST test may over-correct if circles are genuinely part of the suffix region in Brain-V's operational sense but displaced by Stolfi's parsing logic.

The 'e' letter is layer-dependent in Stolfi: isolated e follows the core as part of the core under certain conditions; ee follows as a mantle ligature; other e occurrences may be crust. A uniform 'remove e from CRUST' test does not capture this context dependence.

Even if categorisation drift accounts for 80%+ of the gap, secondary drifts (q, weirdos, suffix definition) may account for the remaining residual. Achieving 0.80 ± 0.05 may require stacking multiple corrections — the single-largest-source identification may be meaningful without being sufficient.

If cumulative reconciliation fails to reach 0.80 ± 0.05, the residual could be attributable to genuine corpus differences (Stolfi's 2000 data vs Brain-V's 2023-2025 EVA transliteration versions) rather than pipeline drift.
test:
LOCKED PROCEDURE.

  DATA: Voynich corpus. Primary test corpus = WHOLE MANUSCRIPT (Hand A + Hand B, all currier_language values), matching Stolfi's scope. Secondary reporting on Hand A alone and Hand B alone for interpretability. Labels are included (Stolfi includes them).

  TOKENISATION (LOCKED, Stolfi-verified):
    MULTI = ['cth', 'cph', 'ckh', 'cfh', 'ch', 'sh', 'ee'], longest-match-first.
    'ol' is NOT a ligature (Stolfi parses o and l as separate letters).
    This matches H-BV-EXTERNAL-PREDICTIONS-01 and H-BV-DENSITY-DIAGNOSIS-01.

  STOLFI-DERIVED REFERENCE (verbatim from voynich.nu/hist/stolfi/grammar.html):
    DEALERS_STOLFI = {d, l, r, s, n, x, i, m, g}  (9 letters)
    CIRCLES_STOLFI = {a, o, y}                    (separate category, parsed as modifiers)
    CORE_STOLFI    = {t, p, k, f, cth, cph, ckh, cfh}
    MANTLE_STOLFI  = {ch, sh, ee}
    Q_NOT_DEALER:  'q counts as 0.4 of a dealer' (Stolfi's own words)
    STOLFI_SUFFIX_REFERENCE = 0.80 (unconditional suffix dealer mean, whole manuscript)
    STOLFI_PREFIX_REFERENCE = 0.14

  BRAIN-V CURRENT REFERENCE (from H-BV-DENSITY-DIAGNOSIS-01):
    CRUST_BV = {d, l, r, s, n, x, i, m, g, q, o, y, a, e}  (14 letters)
    suffix_mean_BV(whole) = to be measured (expected ~2.45 based on A=2.42, B=2.52)

  FIVE SUB-TESTS (S1-S5). Each holds all other choices constant at the BRAIN-V defaults and varies ONE dimension. The cumulative test (S5) applies Stolfi-literal across all dimensions.

  S1 — TOKENISATION SUB-TEST:
    Compute suffix_dealer_mean under each tokenisation variant, keeping CRUST_BV and standard-split and all-tokens otherwise.
      T1a: MULTI = ['cth','cph','ckh','cfh','ch','sh','ee']           (Brain-V / Stolfi literal)
      T1b: MULTI = ['cth','cph','ckh','cfh','ch','sh']                (no 'ee' ligature; sensitivity)
      T1c: MULTI = ['cth','cph','ckh','cfh']                          (core ligatures only; no mantle ligatures)
      T1d: MULTI = []                                                 (no ligatures at all; single-char only)
    REPORT: 4-row table with suffix_mean per variant. Largest delta from T1a is the tokenisation drift contribution.

  S2 — SLOT-ASSIGNMENT SUB-TEST (hypothesised primary driver):
    Compute suffix_dealer_mean under each dealer-set variant, keeping T1a tokenisation and standard-split and all-tokens otherwise.
      S2a: CRUST_BV  = {d,l,r,s,n,x,i,m,g,q,o,y,a,e}  (14 letters, Brain-V current)
      S2b: DEALERS_STOLFI_STRICT = {d,l,r,s,n,x,i,m,g}  (9 letters, Stolfi verbatim, EXCLUDES circles and q)
      S2c: DEALERS_STOLFI_WITH_Q = DEALERS_STOLFI_STRICT + {q}  (adds q; tests whether q is the main difference)
      S2d: DEALERS_MINUS_E_ONLY = CRUST_BV - {e}  (removes only e)
      S2e: DEALERS_MINUS_CIRCLES = CRUST_BV - {a,o,y}  (removes the three circles; keeps e, q)
    REPORT: 5-row table. Largest delta from S2a is the slot-assignment drift contribution.
    PRE-REGISTERED EXPECTATION: S2b (Stolfi strict dealers) should drop the mean to near 0.80.

  S3 — SUFFIX-DEFINITION SUB-TEST:
    Compute suffix_dealer_mean under each suffix definition, keeping T1a tokenisation and CRUST_BV and all-tokens otherwise.
      D3a: 'all glyphs after first core, filtered to CRUST_BV'                 (Brain-V current)
      D3b: 'all glyphs after LAST core'                                       (if multi-core, takes the innermost)
      D3c: 'crust glyphs after core and before any mantle' (strict Stolfi layer: suffix must not cross back into mantle)
      D3d: 'only split-crust words (words with both prefix AND suffix, i.e., core is not at position 0 or length-1)'  (matches Stolfi's 'split crust' table scope)
    REPORT: 4-row table. Largest delta is the suffix-definition drift contribution.

  S4 — TOKEN-FILTERING SUB-TEST:
    Compute suffix_dealer_mean under each filtering variant, keeping T1a / CRUST_BV / D3a otherwise.
      F4a: all tokens                                                         (Brain-V current)
      F4b: exclude q-initial tokens                                           (matches the standard EXTERNAL-PREDICTIONS-01 T2 convention; Stolfi's tables typically report 'without q' columns)
      F4c: exclude tokens containing non-standard glyphs {b,c,j,u,v,z} (Brain-V's 'unknown glyphs')
      F4d: exclude hapaxes (tokens with corpus frequency 1)
      F4e: exclude 'weirdos' (Stolfi's abnormal-word class; operationalised as: tokens containing glyph sequences not parseable under the locked Stolfi grammar, proxied here by 'tokens with non-standard glyphs' + 'tokens of length >= 12' — the long-token heuristic catches common abnormal patterns)
    REPORT: 5-row table.

  S5 — CUMULATIVE STOLFI-LITERAL TEST:
    Apply ALL of: T1a tokenisation + DEALERS_STOLFI_STRICT (S2b) + D3a suffix definition + F4b q-exclusion + F4c non-standard glyph exclusion.
    Compute cumulative_suffix_mean on the whole manuscript.
    Also compute on Hand A alone and Hand B alone for reporting.
    REPORT: cumulative mean, delta to Stolfi's 0.80.

  DECISION RULES (LOCKED):

    LARGEST_SINGLE_SOURCE:
      Compute delta_s = |baseline_mean - variant_mean| for each S1/S2/S3/S4 variant vs its baseline (T1a/S2a/D3a/F4a).
      LARGEST_SINGLE_SOURCE = the sub-test (S1, S2, S3, or S4) containing the variant with the largest delta.
      REPORT VERBATIM.

    CUMULATIVE_REPRODUCTION_PASS iff |cumulative_suffix_mean_whole - 0.80| <= 0.05
    CUMULATIVE_REPRODUCTION_FAIL otherwise.

    VERDICT MAP:
      CUMULATIVE PASS AND S2 is LARGEST_SINGLE_SOURCE: DIAGNOSED_SLOT_ASSIGNMENT. Brain-V's pipeline can be made Stolfi-comparable by adopting DEALERS_STOLFI_STRICT.
      CUMULATIVE PASS AND other sub-test is LARGEST_SINGLE_SOURCE: DIAGNOSED_OTHER_DRIFT. Identify the specific dimension.
      CUMULATIVE FAIL AND within 0.15 of 0.80: PARTIAL_RECONCILIATION. Majority of drift accounted for, residual remains — follow up needed.
      CUMULATIVE FAIL AND outside 0.15: FUNDAMENTAL_DIFFERENCE. Brain-V's corpus or methodology has non-methodological differences from Stolfi's; cannot be reconciled by operational adjustments alone.

    IMPLICATIONS FOR PRIOR TESTS:
      If CUMULATIVE PASS: Brain-V's prior tests comparing against Stolfi's numbers (EXTERNAL-PREDICTIONS-01 T1/T2/T3 and COMPOSITE-OUTER-01 M4) were pipeline-drift tests, not corpus-property tests. They should be re-run under the reconciled operationalisation, with any substantive findings re-derived from those runs.
      If CUMULATIVE FAIL: a fundamental-methodology note is required in v2 — Brain-V's framework cannot directly compare against Stolfi-derived numbers without an explicit translation layer.

  WORKED EXAMPLE (to lock S2b vs S2a distinction):
    Token 'chedy' tokenises as ['ch','e','d','y']. No CORE glyph. Excluded from both S2a and S2b suffix counting (no split possible).
    Token 'chekedy' tokenises as ['ch','e','k','e','d','y']. CORE is 'k' at position 2. Suffix = ['e','d','y'].
      Under S2a (Brain-V CRUST): count of {e,d,y} in {d,l,r,s,n,x,i,m,g,q,o,y,a,e} = 3
      Under S2b (Stolfi STRICT): count of {e,d,y} in {d,l,r,s,n,x,i,m,g} = 1 (only d)
      Reduction for this token: 3 -> 1, factor 3x.
    Token 'qokeedy' tokenises as ['q','o','k','ee','d','y']. q-initial.
      Under F4a (all tokens): included. CORE at position 2. Suffix = ['ee','d','y'].
        S2a count: 'ee' not in CRUST_BV (it's mantle), 'd' yes, 'y' yes -> 2 dealers
        S2b count: 'ee' not in dealers (mantle), 'd' yes, 'y' no -> 1 dealer
      Under F4b (exclude q-initial): excluded entirely.

  WORKED EXAMPLE (to lock S5 cumulative):
    Same token 'chekedy'. T1a tokenisation. CORE 'k'. Suffix ['e','d','y'].
      S5 applies DEALERS_STOLFI_STRICT: count of {e,d,y} in {d,l,r,s,n,x,i,m,g} = 1.
      F4b excludes q-initial (this token is not q-initial, kept).
      F4c excludes non-standard glyphs (none in this token, kept).
      per_token_suffix_dealer = 1.
    Repeat across all qualifying whole-manuscript tokens; compute mean.
test_metric: suffix_dealer_mean under each sub-test variant (S1: 4 variants; S2: 5 variants; S3: 4 variants; S4: 5 variants; S5: 1 cumulative). Largest single source = sub-test with largest delta from BV baseline. Cumulative reproduction tested against Stolfi's 0.80 ± 0.05 on whole manuscript.
test_threshold: LARGEST_SINGLE_SOURCE identified verbatim. CUMULATIVE PASS if |cumulative_mean_whole - 0.80| <= 0.05; FAIL otherwise. Four verdict bins: DIAGNOSED_SLOT_ASSIGNMENT, DIAGNOSED_OTHER_DRIFT, PARTIAL_RECONCILIATION, FUNDAMENTAL_DIFFERENCE.
tests_remaining: locked_stolfi_reconciliation_test: run S1-S5 on whole manuscript + Hand A + Hand B; identify largest drift source; check cumulative reproduction against 0.80 +/- 0.05; report which verdict bin holds
reasoning: H-BV-DENSITY-DIAGNOSIS-01 confirmed that the 3x suffix dealer mean drift between Brain-V and Stolfi is pipeline-wide, not Hand-A-specific. Both hands land near 2.4-2.5 vs Stolfi's 0.80. This hypothesis diagnoses which specific methodology choice is the primary driver. A literature pass on Stolfi's grammar (voynich.nu/hist/stolfi/grammar.html) establishes that Stolfi's strict DEALERS = {d,l,r,s,n,x,i,m,g} — only 9 letters — while Brain-V's CRUST includes 5 extra glyphs {q,o,y,a,e} that Stolfi categorises as CIRCLES or treats with special rules. This is the pre-registered primary drift hypothesis: slot-assignment drift (S2b) should account for the majority of the 3x factor. The other four sub-tests (tokenisation, suffix definition, token filtering, cumulative) isolate secondary drifts and establish whether the cumulative correction reproduces Stolfi's 0.80 within tolerance. If it does, Brain-V's prior comparisons against Stolfi's numbers can be re-run under the reconciled operationalisation. If it does not, v2 must explicitly state that Brain-V's framework cannot directly compare against Stolfi's published numbers without a translation layer. Every measurement has locked worked examples per the methodology lessons from T3, M4, and DENSITY-DIAGNOSIS.
source: Brain-V 2026-04-17 user-directed diagnostic follow-up to H-BV-DENSITY-DIAGNOSIS-01 OPERATIONALISATION_DRIFT verdict. Stolfi categorisation verified verbatim against Stolfi 2000 grammar page (voynich.nu/hist/stolfi/grammar.html) via dedicated literature extraction 2026-04-17.
